subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Region R: f(x)=8x³ and g(x)=sin(πx) — Tangent Line, Area & Rotation Volume (Write Only)
stimulus: Let $R$ be the region in the first quadrant enclosed by the graphs of $f(x) = 8x^3$ and $g(x) = \sin(\pi x)$, as shown in the figure.

parts.prompt: Write an equation for the line tangent to the graph of $f$ at $x = \frac{1}{2}$.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $f'(1/2) = 6$ and $f(1/2) = 1$. Tangent line: $y = 1 + 6(x - \frac{1}{2})$ or equivalent.

rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct integrand: $g(x) - f(x) = \sin(\pi x) - 8x^3$ on $[0, \frac{1}{2}]$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Area $= \int_0^{1/2}(\sin(\pi x) - 8x^3)\, dx = \left[-\frac{1}{\pi}\cos(\pi x) - 2x^4\right]_0^{1/2} = \frac{1}{\pi} - \frac{1}{8}$.

parts.prompt: Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression for the volume of the solid generated when $R$ is rotated about the horizontal line $y = 1$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct limits ($0$ to $\frac{1}{2}$) and constant $\pi$
1 pt: Correct outer radius: $1 - f(x) = 1 - 8x^3$; correct inner radius: $1 - g(x) = 1 - \sin(\pi x)$
1 pt: Correct expression: $\pi\int_0^{1/2}\left[(1-f(x))^2 - (1-g(x))^2\right]\, dx = \pi\int_0^{1/2}\left[(1-8x^3)^2 - (1-\sin(\pi x))^2\right]\, dx$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Volume $= \pi\int_0^{1/2}\left[(1-8x^3)^2 - (1-\sin(\pi x))^2\right]\, dx$ (do not evaluate).
